But the core part of foundations is actually how you set your accounts up for success. And a lot of that comes down to what you're kind of looking to build for your accounts. I have a funneling system for my ad accounts which has four layers. The first layer is prospecting and I call that cold. Second layer is remarketing. Third layer is retargeting and fourth layer is for existing customers. Now the way that I kind of explain this is that I focus the funnel based on how warm the audience is. So I recall recording this demonstration back in 2014 with Facebook in Menlo Park and talking them through how to kind of funnel people in a typical marketing sense into a funnel. In fact I don't know any large scale company that's made that work. If you're a drop shipper, if you're doing print on demand, you're starting to test, you will find success doing that. And you can do a Facebook search in your Facebook newsfeed, do a search at the top for buy now, for different keywords that you'd expect to come up on one of these kinds of ads. ## [03:02 - 06:04]

these ads but then they start to plateau. And the reason is that audiences are made up of different people based on whether they're in market or not. ## [12:09 - 15:11]

And so you can run more direct response ads. But when you start to find that you're plateauing, you need to go broader. You need to start appealing to more people.
